meta_title: Roofing in Palm Beach Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Palm Beach Gardens, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Free estimates!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida
intro:
Palm Beach Gardens is one of Palm Beach County's most desirable communities — a place where carefully landscaped neighborhoods like PGA National, Mirasol, Old Palm, Frenchman's Reserve, and Evergrene set a high bar for curb appeal, property value, and quality of craftsmanship. With average home values hovering around $580,000, homeowners here understand that a roof isn't simply a utilitarian necessity — it's one of the most visible, structurally critical, and financially consequential components of the entire property. When that roof fails, the consequences ripple through every room in the house and every line of your insurance policy. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the same commitment to excellence that this city demands, delivering full roof replacements and precision repairs that are built to last in South Florida's unforgiving climate.

Located roughly five miles from the Atlantic Coast, Palm Beach Gardens sits in a wind zone rated for 170 mph sustained winds under the Florida Building Code. While the city falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that governs Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities, that distinction does not mean Palm Beach Gardens homeowners can afford to be complacent. The code still mandates hurricane-rated roofing systems engineered to withstand the kinds of catastrophic wind events that have struck this region multiple times within living memory. Homeowners in communities like Evergrene and Frenchman's Reserve who still have their original roofs from the 1980s or early 1990s are particularly vulnerable — nearly 30% of homes in Palm Beach Gardens were built before the sweeping 1994 code revisions that followed Hurricane Andrew.

Whether you're a longtime resident of PGA National who watched Hurricane Frances and Hurricane Jeanne tear through the area in the same 2004 season, or a newer homeowner in Mirasol looking to get ahead of the next storm cycle, the decision to invest in a quality roof replacement is one of the smartest financial and safety choices you can make. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and our team brings 60+ years of combined experience to every project — from the initial inspection and permitting to the final nail and clean-up.

section_why.h2: Why Palm Beach Gardens Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roofing
section_why.body:
In a market as discerning as Palm Beach Gardens, homeowners have access to dozens of roofing contractors — but the gap between a licensed professional and a transient storm chaser can mean the difference between a 30-year roof and a 5-year headache. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built on a single foundational principle: do the job right the first time, every time. That's not just a slogan — it's the reason we've earned an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and why more than 500 families throughout Palm Beach County trust us with their most valuable asset.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our labor. Every technician who climbs onto your roof in PGA National or Old Palm is a trained, vetted member of our permanent team — someone who has installed our specific product lines before, understands the nuances of Palm Beach County's permitting process, and is accountable to our quality standards from start to finish. In an industry where subcontracting is commonplace, this commitment to in-house craftsmanship is rare and immensely valuable.

Our team navigates the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division permitting process daily. We know which inspectors are assigned to which zones, what documentation is required for a compliant roofing permit, and how to move projects through the approval pipeline without unnecessary delays. For homeowners in high-demand communities like Frenchman's Reserve or Mirasol, where HOA architectural review boards may also be involved, our experience with the full approval chain — from permit application to final inspection sign-off — means your project doesn't get stalled by paperwork.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is a locally rooted company that serves Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We're not a national franchise unfamiliar with South Florida's unique climate challenges; we live and work in the same communities we serve. When a storm is bearing down on the Treasure Coast and you need your roof addressed urgently, we're the team that answers the phone.

section_history.h2: Palm Beach Gardens Has a Storm History That Demands a Stronger Roof
section_history.body:
The residents of Palm Beach Gardens know better than most that South Florida's hurricane season is not an abstract threat. This city has been struck by nine named storms since 1990, including a devastating back-to-back assault in 2004 when Hurricanes Frances and Jeanne made landfall within three weeks of each other. Frances, a Category 2 storm, caused widespread roof damage across Palm Beach County in early September of that year — and before many homeowners had even completed repairs, Jeanne struck in late September with similar ferocity. The compounding damage from that season exposed tens of thousands of roofs to prolonged moisture intrusion, accelerating deterioration that in many cases still hasn't been fully addressed.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 added another layer of destruction, arriving as a fast-moving storm that generated some of the highest wind gusts ever recorded in Palm Beach County. Wilma's rapid translation speed — it crossed Florida in under five hours — produced a sustained battering effect that peeled back shingles, broke tile systems, and compromised ridge caps and flashing across communities from West Palm Beach to Jupiter. Then, more than a decade later, Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a stark reminder that no roof is permanent. Irma's sprawling wind field stretched hundreds of miles, bringing tropical-storm-to-Category-1 conditions to Palm Beach Gardens even as the center tracked up the state's west coast.

Roofs that survived 2004 and 2005 with only minor visible damage may have sustained hidden structural compromise — saturated decking, failed underlayments, and fatigued fasteners that leave the system vulnerable to the next event. If your home in Evergrene, PGA National, or any other Palm Beach Gardens neighborhood has a roof that predates 2010, a professional inspection by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is strongly advisable before the next hurricane season begins.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Palm Beach Gardens
section_code.body:
Roofing in Palm Beach Gardens is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), which sets rigorous minimum standards for wind resistance, underlayment, fastener patterns, and product approval. While Palm Beach Gardens is not part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward counties closer to the coast — the city still falls within a 170 mph design wind speed zone. This means all roofing products installed must carry valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) numbers, demonstrating that they've been independently tested and certified to withstand the wind pressures generated in this specific zone.

For homeowners in the approximately 30% of Palm Beach Gardens homes built before 1994, the pre-Andrew building standards under which those roofs were originally installed are now considered woefully inadequate by modern benchmarks. A full roof replacement — rather than a patch repair — triggers a mandatory upgrade to current FBC standards, which include enhanced nailing schedules for roof decking (typically 8d nails at 6-inch spacing), self-adhering modified bitumen underlayment or equivalent, and approved secondary water barriers in many configurations. These aren't bureaucratic inconveniences; they're the standards that determine whether your roof survives a direct hit.

All roofing projects in Palm Beach Gardens require permits issued through the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division, followed by mandatory inspections at key stages of the installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission and product documentation to scheduling inspections and securing the final certificate of completion. Our familiarity with the City's Building Division staff and inspection requirements means we don't experience the permit delays that catch less experienced contractors off guard. Every project we complete is fully permitted, code-compliant, and documented — which matters enormously when you file an insurance claim or sell your home.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile Systems: Built for Palm Beach Gardens
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier product lines that are ideally suited to the aesthetic sensibilities and performance demands of Palm Beach Gardens: CertainTeed architectural shingles, led by the renowned Landmark Series, and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both lines carry the Florida Product Approval certifications required for 170 mph wind zone installation, and both represent the upper tier of their respective categories in terms of durability, visual appeal, and long-term value.

The CertainTeed Landmark Series is among the most widely respected architectural shingle products in the country, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles replicate the depth and shadow lines of traditional wood shake or slate without the maintenance demands, and they're engineered to resist wind uplift, algae growth (a persistent challenge in South Florida's humidity), and impact damage from wind-driven debris. For homeowners in neighborhoods like Evergrene or Frenchman's Reserve where HOA guidelines require a specific aesthetic, the Landmark Series offers a broad palette of colors and profiles that satisfy both architectural review boards and insurance underwriters alike. CertainTeed's SureStart Plus warranty program also provides robust coverage that transfers to new owners — a meaningful selling point in Palm Beach Gardens's active real estate market.

For the Mediterranean and Spanish Colonial architecture that defines communities like Mirasol and Old Palm, West Lake's concrete and clay tile systems are the natural choice. These products deliver the barrel tile profiles and earthy colorations that complement South Florida's resort-style architectural vocabulary while providing superior wind resistance and thermal performance. Concrete tile in particular offers exceptional longevity — 40 to 50 years under normal conditions — and clay tile systems are even more durable, routinely lasting 50 years or more with proper maintenance. Both West Lake tile options are tested and approved for Palm Beach County's wind requirements and are available in a range of profiles, finishes, and weight classes to accommodate the structural specifications of your existing home.

section_cost.h2: What Does a Roof Replacement Cost in Palm Beach Gardens — and How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
For a home in Palm Beach Gardens with an average value of around $580,000, a full roof replacement is a significant but highly strategic investment. Typical costs for a residential roof replacement in this area range from approximately $15,000 to $45,000 or more, depending on roof size, pitch, material selection, and the complexity of penetrations, valleys, and architectural details. Architectural shingle systems using CertainTeed Landmark products generally represent the more accessible end of the investment spectrum, while West Lake clay tile installations on larger estates in communities like Old Palm or Mirasol will fall at the higher end. In every case, the return on that investment — through enhanced storm protection, insurance savings, and property value — is well-documented.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed to make high-quality roofing accessible without financial strain. Qualified homeowners can begin their project with $0 down and finance monthly payments starting as low as $87 per month. Our financing partnerships are structured to accommodate a range of credit profiles and project sizes, so whether you're replacing a modest shingle roof on a garden home near The Gardens Mall or commissioning a full tile replacement on a 5,000-square-foot estate in Frenchman's Reserve, we can structure a payment plan that fits your budget and timeline.

Beyond the financing options, it's important to understand the financial opportunity cost of delaying a roof replacement. A deteriorating roof in Palm Beach Gardens doesn't just risk storm damage — it opens you to denied insurance claims, loss of coverage altogether, and deferred maintenance costs that compound over time. Conversely, a new code-compliant roof can generate immediate and ongoing returns through reduced insurance premiums (often 20–40% on the wind portion of your homeowner's policy), improved energy efficiency from modern underlayment systems, and a meaningful boost to resale value in one of Palm Beach County's most competitive real estate markets.

section_insurance.h2: Roofing Upgrades That Can Significantly Lower Your Palm Beach Gardens Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Palm Beach County has become one of the most significant ongoing costs of homeownership in the region — a reality that's acutely felt in high-value communities like PGA National and Old Palm, where annual premiums can represent a meaningful percentage of total housing costs. The good news for Palm Beach Gardens homeowners is that a properly permitted, hurricane-rated roof replacement is among the most effective tools available for reducing those premiums, often generating savings that contribute materially to offsetting the cost of the project itself.

The mechanism for these savings is the wind mitigation inspection — a formal assessment conducted by a licensed inspector that evaluates your roof's construction features against a standardized set of criteria developed by Florida's Office of Insurance Regulation. Factors such as roof shape (hip roofs earn the best credits), secondary water resistance (the self-adhering underlayment used in modern FBC-compliant installations), roof deck attachment (the enhanced nailing schedules now required by code), and roof-to-wall connections all factor into the credits your insurer applies to the wind portion of your premium. A new Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof installation, completed to current FBC standards with approved products, typically qualifies for the highest available credits in each of these categories.

Major carriers operating in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all utilize the OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation form to calculate these discounts, and the savings can be substantial: many homeowners report reductions of 20% to 40% on the wind premium component, which in Palm Beach County can represent several hundred to several thousand dollars annually. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates directly with wind mitigation inspectors and provides complete documentation packages to expedite the insurance review process. We also recommend that every customer obtain a fresh wind mitigation inspection immediately upon project completion — not after the next renewal cycle — to begin capturing those savings as quickly as possible.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Palm Beach Gardens home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on several factors: the age of your current roof, the extent and location of damage, and the type of roofing system installed. Homes with roofs older than 20 years — particularly those in PGA National or Evergrene that may predate the 1994 building code reforms — are often better candidates for full replacement than repair. A repair on a compromised decking system or a failing underlayment simply masks deeper problems. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free professional inspections to give you an honest assessment before any money changes hands.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement cost in Palm Beach Gardens and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on roof size, material, and complexity, but most full replacements in Palm Beach Gardens range from approximately $15,000 to $45,000+. CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingle systems typically represent the lower end of that range, while West Lake clay or concrete tile installations on larger homes will be higher. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers $0 down financing with monthly payments starting at $87/month for qualified homeowners, making it possible to begin your project without a large upfront expenditure.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Palm Beach Gardens, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes — all roof replacements in Palm Beach Gardens require a permit issued by the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division, along with mandatory inspections at key installation milestones. Attempting to replace a roof without a permit is a code violation that can result in fines, a requirement to remove the work, and serious complications when selling your home or filing an insurance claim. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit application and inspection scheduling process on your behalf, so you never have to navigate the Building Division alone.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Palm Beach Gardens?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in Palm Beach Gardens are completed within one to three days of active installation, depending on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, and weather conditions. However, the full project timeline — from contract signing to permit issuance to final inspection — typically spans two to four weeks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's familiarity with the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division and our in-house installation team helps us minimize unnecessary delays at every stage of the process.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new code-compliant roof with proper underlayment, an approved nailing schedule, and hurricane-rated products qualifies for substantial wind mitigation credits under Florida's OIR-B1-1802 inspection program. Palm Beach County homeowners with qualifying roofs routinely see 20–40% reductions on the wind portion of their premiums. Given the size of those premiums in this market, the annual savings can meaningfully offset the cost of the roof over time. We recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection immediately after your new roof is completed.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Palm Beach Gardens Home with a Premier Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free inspection and estimate — our in-house experts are ready to help you choose the right CertainTeed or West Lake roofing system for your home, navigate the permitting process seamlessly, and start saving on your insurance premiums from day one.
